title: Aldama obtusifolia
intro: Aldama obtusifolia is a perennial herbaceous plant, commonly known as Hierba de la Santa Maria, that is native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its potential in treating various health conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Aldama obtusifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Aldama obtusifolia is often used to prepare infusions, teas, and topical creams, making it a popular ingredient in various herbal preparations.
taxonomy: Aldama obtusifolia is classified within the family Rosaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. This family includes a wide range of species, from roses and apples to apricots and pears. The genus Aldama is a relatively small group of flowering plants native to the Americas. Aldama obtusifolia is specifically native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a shrubby plant that produces small white flowers in the late spring and early summer.
uses: Aldama obtusifolia, a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, has been reported to have various medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its leaves and stems are also used to treat digestive issues and as a treatment for respiratory problems. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of these uses. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its bioactive compounds, which are being studied for their potential health benefits.

preparations: Aldama obtusifolia is often used in traditional medicine, and herbal preparations based on its leaves and roots are used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are exploited in its herbal preparations.
